title: Bretton Woods and 1971
description: The 1971 suspension of dollar-gold convertibility marked the transition to the modern fiat era. This event is central to debates about monetary discretion, inflation, and hard-money alternatives.

resources.title: Nixon Shock
resources.notes: Overview of the 1971 policy shift and global monetary consequences.

resources.title: Federal Reserve History: Closing the Gold Window
resources.notes: Institutional perspective on the end of convertibility.

resources.title: The Ascent of Money
resources.notes: Long-run history framing before and after Bretton Woods.
